name: Italian Style Pasta Pan
directions: Put your pasta to cook according to package instructions.
While it cooks, prepare your eggplant.
Saute eggplant in the olive oil until soft, adding 1/2 c. water little by little
Then add the pepper,tomato,onion,garlic and all the seasonings.
Let it cook until vegetables are soft and you have a nice sauce.
When the sauce thickens,drain the pasta and add it to the eggplant, folding it in, mixing well.
Add the topping, toss and serve.

name: Hearty "Meat" Skillet
directions: Thaw your"chicken" tenders at room temp.
Meantime, process the rest of your ingredients (cut, chop, seed, etc.).
Cook your rice or pasta (if you are not using leftoverts).
In a large skillet, sautée the onions, peppers and tomatoes, in the olive oil, when beginning to be tender, add the"meats",and the rest of the ingredients, except the tofu.
Cook, stirring occasionally, till all your vegetables are tender, about 15 minutes.
Add the tofu and stir again to blend the flavors well.
Cook for aobut 10 more minutes.
Serve over plain pre-cooked rice or pasta or your choice.

name: Orange Coriander Vinegar
directions: Sterilze a 1 qt glass jar. Use a new lid.
Carefully wash  the Orange and with knife of zesting tool remove the zest into a long sprial.
Place zest and seeds in still warm prepared jar.
Heat vinegar to just below boiling. About 195°F Pour over berries using a funnel. Be careful not to burn yourself.
Wipe lip of jar with clean damp paper towel and seal according to manufacturer instructions. Store in a cool dark place for 3 weeks.
To check flavor pour a drop of vinegar on a wooden spoon or a piece of white bread.
When it is right strain the vinegar through a cheesecloth into pitcher or measuring cup.
Discard the zest and seeds.
Prepare a sterile 1 pt glass bottle or jar and a cork or lid. Pour into the still warm container and wipe lid again with clean paper towel. cork and seal.
This will last up to 3 months if you keep in a cool dark place.
Once open refrigerate.

name: Polenta Pizzas
directions: Slice polenta rounds--about 1/4 inch thick.  Put veggie mix and parmesan cheese on top of sliced polenta rounds and heat at 350~ until very hot.

name: Vegan Blueberry Coffeecake
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Spray an 8-inch sq. baking dish or a 9 inch rectangular pan.
In a small bowl combine the oats, brown sugar and cinnamon; set aside. Let the margarine melt in the oven, microwave or stove top.
Combine together the flour, sugar, baking powder, salt and baking soda in a large bowl.
In another bowl mix the melted butter, vinegar, extracts, applesauce, soy milk, lemon juice and vinegar and whisk until well blended.
Stir the wet ingredients into dry ingredients until just moistened. Fold in blueberries. Do not thaw the blueberries.
Pour the batter into the pan, and sprinkle the oat mixture over top.

Bake 25-30 min, or until a toothpick comes out clean. Remove the cake from the pan after 10 minutes of cooling (easiest to do in the refrigerator) and, if possible, set on a wire rack to cool completely.

name: Clean Eating Coffee Creamer
directions: In a blender mix all ingredients until well blended.
Pour in a container of choice  and keep it in the fridge.
It's lower in calories than the stuff you buy in the store.  The stuff I had was  50 calories per 1 tbsp ( if you want creamer usually you don't use just 1 tbsp) and this is  50 calories for 1/4 cup.
